idea-plugin.name: Simplified Annotations
idea-plugin.description:
Two static-analysis annotations for Java - @ResourcePath and @XContract - each backed by IDE inspections. No runtime code is generated, and no bytecode is rewritten; everything runs in the editor.

@ResourcePath

  • Resource Path inspection - verifies that every annotated field, parameter, or method return resolves to a file that exists in the project's content source roots (or in user-configured additional roots). Supports an optional base attribute that prefixes the validated path.
  • Resource Path Base-Prefix Usage inspection - catches the common runtime mistake of passing a @ResourcePath(base="X") parameter raw into a resource-loading call (Class.getResourceAsStream, new File(...), etc.) without prepending X/. Offers a quick-fix. Also flags base mismatches across method boundaries.
  • Value resolution follows literals, concatenation, final/static/enum fields, local variables, method-call return values (recursively), and enum-constructor bindings, with bounded recursion and result size.
  • Settings: custom resource-root paths, glob-based file exclusions, separate severity for invalid-base errors.

@XContract

  • Superset of JetBrains @Contract with a richer grammar: relational comparisons, && / || with grouping, named-parameter references, instanceof conditions, typed throws returns, chained comparisons (0 <= index < size()), and full pure / mutates support.
  • A built-in InferredAnnotationProvider synthesises an equivalent @Contract for every @XContract method, so IntelliJ's data-flow analysis works from a single annotation.
  • XContract inspection validates contract syntax, parameter references, type references, mutates tokens, and flags overrides that weaken a super method's contract.
  • XContract Violation at Call Site inspection warns when a call's literal arguments deterministically trigger a fail or throws clause.
